---
title: "PilotStudy"
format: html
author: "Julia Kristensen and Ilia Janfeshan"
---

# Libraries and Reading Data
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)

data <- read_csv("pilot.csv")

data$CookingMethod <- factor(data$CookingMethod)
data$DryingMethod  <- factor(data$DryingMethod)
data$Replicate <- factor(data$Replicate)

data <- data %>%
  group_by(Replicate, DryingMethod, CookingMethod) %>%
  summarise(NumQuarters = mean(NumQuarters), .groups = "drop")
```

## Finding Standard Deviation of the SP Factor and the WP Factor
```{r}
fit <- lmer(NumQuarters ~ CookingMethod * DryingMethod +
              (1 | Replicate), data)

sp_anova_fit <- anova(fit)
MSE_sp <- sp_anova_fit$`Mean Sq`[3] / sp_anova_fit$`F value`[3]

# One-Way ANOVA on plot totals
plot_tots <- data %>%
  group_by(Replicate, DryingMethod) %>%
  summarise(NumQuarters = mean(NumQuarters), .groups = "drop")

wp_ow_anova_fit <- anova(lm(NumQuarters ~ DryingMethod, plot_tots))
MSE_wp <- wp_ow_anova_fit$`Mean Sq`[2]

# c = 1 / number of sub-plots per whole-plot
c <- 0.5

(sd_wp <- sqrt(MSE_wp - (c * MSE_sp)))
(sd_sp <- sqrt(MSE_sp))
```

## Choosing effect sizes
```{r}
set.seed(403)

# Cooking Effect Size: 
mu <- c(10,-10)

# Drying Effect Size: Because our difference in wait time is large (22 hrs), a difference of only +-20 quarters is considered a significant enough difference. 
beta <- c(15,-15)

# Interaction Effect Size:
gamma <- c(15,-15)

reps <- 1:4

for (n in reps){
  for (i in 1:2){
    Plot <- Plot + 1
    WP_err <- rnorm(n = 1, mean = 0, sd = sd_wp)
  }
  for (j in 1:2){
    Yij <- 
  }
}
```
